title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy software giants by consolidating finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She argues that even innovative companies like Google rely on inadequate tools like spreadsheets for strategic planning, and that building a complete enterprise platform from day one while leveraging customer trust from respected companies is more effective than incremental feature releases.
writeup: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, explains how the company's AI performance management platform competes against far larger legacy software vendors by consolidating finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data in one place. She notes that even companies like Google still rely on spreadsheets for strategic planning, framing those inadequate tools as the opening her platform targets. The core argument: building a complete enterprise platform from day one, rather than shipping incremental features, and leveraging the trust earned from respected customers is the more effective path to displacing entrenched giants.
